about.title: Pixel voyage
about.agencyDescription: Pixel voyage is an avant-garde agency specializing in the creation and management of interactive digital art exhibitions. We combine advanced software engineering skills with deep curatorial sensitivity to transform historical archives and physical collections into immersive experiences accessible worldwide.

data.famousWork: Le Baiser de l'Hôtel de Ville
data.subject: Parisian life, Children, Love
data.originsP1: Born in Gentilly, Doisneau transformed the streets of Paris into his stage. After training as a lithographer, he found his true voice capturing fleeting moments of beauty in the daily life of the working class.
data.originsP2: His ability to find enchantment in the ordinary made him the pillar of French humanist photography, celebrating humanity with a benevolent and never cynical gaze.
data.keyProjectP1: Through thousands of shots, he documented the transformation of Paris. His work is not just a chronicle, but a visual narrative that blends reality and a subtle poetic staging.
data.keyProjectP2: The famous shot 'The Kiss' has become the universal symbol of Parisian romance, embodying Doisneau's ability to crystallize pure emotion in an instant.
data.philosophyDesc: For Doisneau, taking photographs was not a mechanical act, but a search for 'little lights' in the darkness of routine. He believed the world was full of daily miracles ready to be discovered.
data.iconDesc: He left over 450,000 negatives that tell of an era of hope and simplicity, influencing generations of street photographers worldwide.
data.legacyP1: His images continue to define the collective imagination of Paris. Doisneau did not seek technical perfection, but the emotional truth hidden in a smile or a children's game.
data.legacyP2: His legacy lies in the gentleness of his gaze, a constant reminder that beauty resides in the simplicity of human encounters.

data.subject: Identity, Borders, Women
data.originsP1: A mathematician by training, Arthur applies analytical rigor to deeply human narratives. Her work explores the intersections between cultures, with a particular focus on the private lives of women.
data.originsP2: Her career has taken her from Eastern Europe to Saudi Arabia, always seeking to overcome visual barriers to show often inaccessible worlds.
data.keyProjectP1: In 'Jeddah Diary', she documents the lives of young women in Saudi Arabia. In a context where photography is often forbidden, she uses flash and shadows to protect the subjects while revealing their reality.
data.keyProjectP2: The project is a meditation on visibility and the power of the unspoken, redefining the canons of traditional photojournalism through an artistic approach.
data.philosophyDesc: Her work bridges the gap between document and art, focusing on the personal implications of geographical and cultural boundaries and the technological evolution of the human body.
data.iconDesc: A Magnum member since 2013, Arthur continues to push the documentary medium towards new expressive forms, including experiments with AI and robotics.
data.legacyP1: Through her 'Fishbar' space in London and her global projects, she supports a vision of photography that is both analytical and vulnerable.
data.legacyP2: She remains a fundamental voice in defining how the image can tell the complexity of modern identity in the age of globalization.

data.subject: Memory, Loss, History
data.originsP1: Leaving Japan in 1973, Furuya crossed Europe, settling in Austria. His work is a bridge between Eastern culture and the European documentary aesthetics of the Cold War.
data.originsP2: His photography became a life diary the moment he began to obsessively portray his wife Christine, creating an archive of love and pain.
data.keyProjectP1: After Christine's death, Furuya dedicated his life to reworking the photos taken together. 'Mémoires' is not just a tribute, but a cathartic process of reconstructing memory.
data.keyProjectP2: The images mix personal tragedy with the historical changes of Europe, making individual pain a universal collective experience.
data.philosophyDesc: Photography for Furuya is the only tool to fight oblivion. His images change meaning every time they are rearranged, reflecting the mutable nature of memory.
data.iconDesc: His photobooks are editing masterpieces, where every juxtaposition between past and present creates new layers of emotional truth.
data.legacyP1: Furuya redefined autobiographical photography, showing how the personal archive can become a powerful and heartbreaking art form.
data.legacyP2: His work teaches that photography does not stop time, but allows us to dialogue with what we have lost.

data.subject: Subcultures, Illusions, Space
data.originsP1: With a background in Graphic Design, Moshammer creates images that explode with color and composition. Her vision moves away from realism to explore surface and illusion.
data.originsP2: Her visual narratives are hybrid, mixing captured reality and aesthetic construction to question the way we perceive places and people.
data.keyProjectP1: In this project, Las Vegas is not a city, but a mirage. Moshammer explores the promises of the American dream through vibrant portraits and almost abstract urban details.
data.keyProjectP2: Her use of saturated color transforms the everyday into something alien, revealing the loneliness and artificial beauty of marginal contexts.
data.philosophyDesc: She investigates how the environment shapes identity. For her, photography is not proof of truth, but a means to create new realities charged with emotional and graphic tension.
data.iconDesc: Awarded as a FOAM Talent, she represents the new avant-garde that blends photography, fashion, and social critique into a unique visual language.
data.legacyP1: Moshammer challenges the static nature of the documentary genre, inviting the viewer to get lost in a world where the boundary between true and false is deliberately blurred.
data.legacyP2: Her work is a testament to the vitality of contemporary photography in constantly reinventing its gaze on the world.
